edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: The New Jersey faction of the Lucchese crime family, also known as the Jersey Crew, is a powerful crew of Italian-American mobsters which operates throughout the Northern New Jersey area.
edges.context: The New Jersey faction is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Gambino, Genovese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia
edges.context: The infobox lists Genovese as an ally of the New Jersey faction.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: the relationship between Philadelphia and the New York Families, especially the Luccheses, eventually turned worse after the murder of Angelo Bruno, which led to all cooperation between the families being completely terminated.
edges.context: Cooperation between the New Jersey faction and Philadelphia ended after Bruno's murder.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: The Commission decided to disband the Newark family, while declaring Newark an open city and dividing the family's territory among the Five Families of New York.
edges.context: The Profaci family was one of the Five Families that received territory after the Newark family was disbanded.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: It also revealed that members of the Lucchese family's New Jersey faction had an alliance with a New Jersey corrections officer and members of the Nine Trey Gangster's (a set of the Bloods) to smuggle drugs and pre-paid cell phones into East Jersey State Prison in Woodbridge.
edges.context: The New Jersey faction allied with Nine Trey Gangsters to smuggle drugs and phones into prison.

edges.evidence_quote: members of the Nine Trey Gangster's (a set of the Bloods)
edges.context: Nine Trey Gangsters are a set of the Bloods.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2008, Martin Taccetta was indicted along with Gambino crime family capo Andrew Merola on racketeering, gambling and labor corruption.
edges.context: Members of the New Jersey faction and Gambino family were indicted together, indicating cooperation.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: the crew reported to Caporegime Domenico Cutaia who also controlled the Vario-Cutaia Crew
edges.context: The New Jersey faction reported to a capo who also controlled the Vario-Cutaia Crew, indicating structural tie.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: the crew reported to Caporegime John 'Johnny Hooks' Capra who also controlled his Harlem-Bronx crew
edges.context: The New Jersey faction reported to a capo who also controlled the Harlem-Bronx crew, indicating structural tie.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: George 'Georgie Neck' Zappola – stepped down and became captain of the Brooklyn Faction
edges.context: A former leader of the New Jersey faction became captain of the Brooklyn Faction, indicating structural tie.
